summary: Fixed the blocking final-review issue by changing scripts/build-images.sh to build scion-ops-new-ui-eval with REPO_ROOT as the Docker context, matching Taskfile build:new-ui-eval and the Dockerfile's repo-root-relative COPY new-ui-evaluation/... paths. Docker was unavailable in this environment, so verification used syntax, whitespace, and static context/source checks as requested.
tasks_completed: Read existing new-ui-eval build wiring and session findings; no separate final-review artifact was present in the session findings directory.
Aligned scripts/build-images.sh new-ui-eval image build context to the repository root so image-build/new-ui-eval/Dockerfile COPY new-ui-evaluation/... sources resolve.
Kept Taskfile build:new-ui-eval and image-build/new-ui-eval/Dockerfile consistent with the repo-root Docker context pairing.
Committed and pushed the repair branch.
tests_run:
bash -lc 'set -e; context=$(awk -F" '''/NEW_UI_EVAL_CONTEXT=/ {print $2}''' scripts/build-images.sh | tail -1); test "$context" = "\$REPO_ROOT"; printf "script context: %s
" "$context"; shopt -s nullglob; files=(new-ui-evaluation/package*.json); ((${#files[@]})); printf "ok %s
" "${files[@]}"; test -d new-ui-evaluation; printf "ok new-ui-evaluation/
"'

rg -n "docker build --tag '\{\{\.SCION_OPS_NEW_UI_EVAL_IMAGE\}\}' --file image-build/new-ui-eval/Dockerfile \.$" Taskfile.yml && printf 'ok Taskfile build:new-ui-eval uses repo-root context with image-build/new-ui-eval/Dockerfile\n'
command -v docker >/dev/null && docker --version || printf 'docker unavailable; Docker build intentionally not required for this repair\n'
